---
title: "Building Slides with Quarto"
subtitle: "Teaching session guide (2h30)"
author: "Your Name"
date: last-modified
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry:
      - margin=2cm
    colorlinks: true
---

## Before students arrive

- Confirm every machine has Quarto, a LaTeX distribution with `lualatex`
  (`quarto install tinytex`), and R with `knitr` installed.
- Have everyone `git pull` (or clone) `quarto-thesis-um6ss` so
  `student-starter.qmd` and the rest of the repo are current.
- Your own setup: `quarto-slides-course.qmd` open in an editor (present
  from / type into this live), its rendered `quarto-slides-course.pdf`
  open in a viewer, and a second window with the real thesis repo files
  (`_quarto.yml`, `Chapters/Chapter2.qmd`, `R/shared-results.R`,
  `slides.qmd`) for the thesis-document segment.
- Tell everyone to open `student-starter.qmd` in RStudio, not the New
  Quarto Presentation wizard.

## Timed agenda (150 minutes)

| Time | Block | Slides / activity |
|---|---|---|
| 0:00-0:10 | Welcome + Getting started | Welcome, Session outline, What is Quarto, The YAML header, Slides are just level-2 headings. Conceptual only, no typing. |
| 0:10-0:18 | Text and structure | Headings/lists/emphasis, Speaker notes. Students add a second slide + a `{.notes}` block. |
| 0:18-0:38 | Images and figures | Insert a static image, Insert a figure from code, Resize at render time (`fig-width`/`fig-height`), Resize displayed output (`out-width`). **Render checkpoint 1.** |
| 0:38-0:48 | Code and its output | Display code (`echo: true`), Sourcing shared code (`source()` + `include: false`). |
| 0:48-0:56 | Tables | Tables from code (`knitr::kable()`), Markdown tables. Brisk; recover time here if behind. |
| 0:56-1:06 | **Break** | |
| 1:06-1:21 | Layout and emphasis | Incremental reveals, Two-column layouts, Callout blocks. **Render checkpoint 2.** |
| 1:21-1:29 | Citations and cross-references | `[@key]` citations, `@fig-.../@tbl-.../@eq-...` cross-refs, auto-generated `#refs` reference list. |
| 1:29-1:37 | Math and footnotes | Inline/display equations with `{#eq-...}`, footnotes. **Render checkpoint 3.** |
| 1:37-1:57 | The thesis document (live, real repo) | `_quarto.yml` metadata, add/edit a chapter, show `R/shared-results.R` called from `Chapters/Chapter2.qmd`, render to `UM6SS-Thesis-Template.pdf`. |
| 1:57-2:05 | **Break** | |
| 2:05-2:25 | Using this for your own defense | Start from `slides.qmd`, reuse figures from thesis chapters, checklist before presenting, References, Closing. |
| 2:25-2:30 | Wrap-up / questions | |

**If running behind**, cut in this order: Tables -> Callout blocks ->
Markdown tables. Never cut: the `fig-width`/`out-width` pair, the
thesis-document segment, or "Using this for your own defense."

\newpage

## Reference: files that parametrize the rendered PDFs

### Thesis PDF (`UM6SS-Thesis-Template.pdf`)

| File | What it controls |
|---|---|
| `_quarto.yml` | Title, author, supervisor, degree, university, abstract, chapter order, `quarto-thesis-pdf` format options (`urlcolor`, `linkcolor`, `toclinkcolor`, `toc-depth`), bibliography file |
| `_extensions/quarto-thesis/_extension.yml` | Defines the `quarto-thesis-pdf` format: which `.cls` to use, classoption, geometry/margins, which partials to inject and where |
| `_extensions/quarto-thesis/MastersDoctoralThesis.cls` | The LaTeX document class: page layout, chapter/section styling, declaration/abstract/acknowledgements environments |
| `_extensions/quarto-thesis/partials/in-header.tex` | LaTeX preamble additions: `UM6SSRed`/`UM6SSDarkRed`/`UM6SSSoftRed` colors, name/degree placeholders, `biblatex` setup, PDF metadata |
| `_extensions/quarto-thesis/partials/before-body.tex` | Everything before Chapter 1: title page, declaration/quotation/abstract/acknowledgements, table of contents + list of figures/tables, abbreviations/symbols/dedication |
| `_extensions/quarto-thesis/partials/definitions.tex` | Small `include-before-body` snippet (auxiliary macros) |
| `_extensions/quarto-thesis/partials/title.tex` | Pointer comment only; title logic actually lives in `before-body.tex` |
| `Frontmatter/*.tex` | Declaration, acknowledgements, abbreviations, symbols, dedication text, referenced by path from the `thesis:` block in `_quarto.yml` |
| `example.bib` | Bibliography content, resolved via `bibliography:` in `_quarto.yml` |

### Beamer decks (`slides.pdf`, `quarto-slides-course.pdf`)

| File | What it controls |
|---|---|
| YAML header inside each `.qmd` | `format: beamer` options: theme, colortheme, aspectratio, navigation, `include-in-header` |
| `slides-in-header.tex` | Re-defines `UM6SSRed`/`UM6SSDarkRed`/`UM6SSSoftRed` independently, `\setbeamercolor` for palette/structure/title/frametitle, bullet styling, `\titlegraphic{}` (the logo) |

### Shared across both

- `images/logo.png` -- referenced by path from `thesis.logo` in
  `_quarto.yml` and from `\titlegraphic` in `slides-in-header.tex`.
- `R/shared-results.R` -- not a rendering-config file, but both the
  thesis chapter and the slide decks call into it, so its content
  silently determines what figures/tables appear in both outputs.

**Note**: `UM6SSRed` and its variants are defined twice (once in
`in-header.tex`, once in `slides-in-header.tex`), not in one shared
color file. Rebranding requires editing both.
